export const meta = {
  name: 'consolidate-the-one-plan',
  description: 'Merge all six adversarial verdicts, the judge oracle, and every correction into ONE executable plan — then hand it to the wheel',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Consolidate', detail: 'three independent consolidators, different priorities' },
    { title: 'Attack', detail: 'attack the consolidation itself' },
    { title: 'Final', detail: 'the one plan' },
  ],
}

const PP = '/home/polaris/polaris_project'
const FW = '/home/polaris/wt/flywheel'
const F = `${PP}/sota_review/foundation`

const GROUND = `
# READ THESE FIRST, IN FULL
- ${F}/ALL_VERDICTS.md   — all SIX adversarial verdicts + the adjudication. Everything here has been ATTACKED.
- ${F}/FINAL_PLAN.md     — the adjudicator's plan.
- ${F}/SOL_PLAN_V4.md, ${F}/FABLE_PLAN_V4.md, ${F}/OPERATOR_FIXES.md, ${F}/OPUS_SYNTHESIS_V1.md — the inputs.

# THE MEASURED TRUTH (all from the judge's OWN written output — scripts/judge_feedback.py)
| system | insight | compreh | instr | read | OVERALL |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| cellcog (#1) | 9.53 | 9.48 | 9.24 | 9.11 | **0.5603** (MEASURED today, not estimated) |
| bodhi | 8.84 | 9.02 | 8.99 | 8.60 | 0.5441 |
| **POLARIS** | 6.45 | 7.22 | 6.11 | **4.71** | **0.4382** |
Weights: INSIGHT .32 | COMPREH .29 | INSTR .25 | READ .14.

**R IS NOT A CONSTANT — PROVEN.** The SAME reference article scores 8.033 against us, 7.545 against bodhi,
7.363 against cellcog. The judge marks the reference DOWN as the target improves. Proof without statistics: if R
were fixed at 8.033, cellcog's 0.5603 would require T = 10.24/10. Impossible. cellcog scored it. Therefore R fell.
=> **Every improvement pays TWICE. Our weak report is currently INFLATING our opponent's score.**

**THE HONEST BAR: our weighted mean across all 25 criteria must go 6.35 -> 9.63/10 — ABOVE cellcog's 9.38.**
That needs ~13 stacked levers. cellcog is not winning on a trick; it is an outstanding report.

# WHAT THE JUDGE SAYS IT PAYS FOR (its own words, measured — not inferred)
- **"explicitly states an evidence constraint to cite only journal articles"** — bodhi +5.0 raw, cellcog +4.0 raw.
  **We score 1.5/10; the REFERENCE scores 2.0.** Highest headroom on the board. **BUT NOTE THE UNITS ERROR THAT WAS
  IN OUR OWN BRIEF: +5.0 is RAW CRITERION POINTS. In SCORE units it is +0.0093 — at the resolvable threshold, not
  a headline lever.** Price everything in SCORE units.
- "compares seminal theory, causal studies, meta-analyses, field experiments, administrative data, qualitative work"
  (evidence-TYPE diversity, not more papers) — +2.6/+3.1 raw
- **"its eight tagged syntheses ... genuine synthesis beyond summary" — cellcog scored 9.8/10**
- "especially effective in reconciling conflicting evidence" — +2.4/+2.7 raw
- On US: *"weak paragraph cohesion... extremely long paragraphs... a fragmented narrative"* (-4.7, our biggest loss)
  and *"some claims are difficult to attribute because no formal citations are provided"*

# WHAT SURVIVED ADVERSARIAL ATTACK
- **THE CHANNEL DEFECT.** RACE's cleaner deletes our 345 [n] markers and our reference list before the judge reads a
  word — leaving 3 journal names and 11 CONSULTANCY names visible. **We are graded on a corpus we did not submit.**
  Our 1.5/10 on the journal criterion is a CHANNEL defect, not a corpus defect.
- **READABILITY IS NOT WORTHLESS.** The 7 readability criteria taken to cellcog's level = **+0.0270 with the R-drag —
  2.9x the resolvable effect.** (The "document shape is worth zero" claim was a UNITS ERROR: the beta was per SD of a
  RESIDUALIZED regressor. Correctly scaled, log(words) = +0.0211/SD, t=5.0.)
- **BUT: sections/H3 ARE a well-powered null** (+0.0020/SD). "Add 30+ subsections" is DEAD. The H3 r=+0.519 that
  everyone read as a mandate is 100% SYSTEM IDENTITY. **And length SATURATES at ~8,000 words — it is a FLOOR
  (~5,000w), not a lever. We are at 9,194, past the knee.**
- **THE OVERFIT IS IN THE OUTLINE, NOT THE TERM LISTS.** \`multi_section_generator.py:785 _ALLOWED_SECTIONS\` hardcodes
  a DRUG-TRIAL genre (Efficacy/Safety/Regulatory/Dose Response). **AND THE FIX IS ALREADY BUILT AND UNWIRED:**
  \`config/domain_packs/{clinical,economics,general,policy,science,technology}.yaml\` each own a \`sections:\` list.
- **GENERALITY IS UNMEASURED, NOT DISPROVEN.** The "0.263" was five ABORTED runs, five weeks stale. But: **38 scored
  runs since 07-12, ALL task 72, zero on anything else.** We do not know what we score on an unseen question.
- **FABRICATION AND INSIGHT OCCUPY THE SAME CELL.** Both are non-entailed relations over true particulars. The
  contract's premise-independent rules reject **97% of cellcog's 9.8/10 prose.** Entailment CANNOT separate them.
- **★ THE INVARIANT (the resolution): EVERY SENTENCE IS EITHER ATTRIBUTED OR OWNED.**
  ATTRIBUTED = names a source; **must be ENTAILED by that source's own verbatim span**; carries the numbers.
  OWNED = the reviewer's voice; **may not name a source**; no new particulars; contradiction-screened;
  **EXPLICITLY PERMITTED TO BE NON-ENTAILED — because that is what insight IS.**
  **FABRICATION = an ATTRIBUTED sentence its source does not entail. INSIGHT = an OWNED sentence its premises do not
  entail. SAME LOGICAL SHAPE — distinguished by WHOSE VOICE, not by entailment.**

# ALREADY BUILT AND WORKING (do not re-plan these; USE them)
- \`scripts/judge_feedback.py\` — THE ORACLE. Captures the judge's per-criterion scores AND written analysis, for
  both articles. The harness discards it. **This is how we stopped guessing.**
- \`scripts/criterion_ab.py\` — criterion-level A/B. **20 of 25 criteria cannot clear a scalar kill rule even at 10/10;
  a lever must be judged on THE CRITERION IT TARGETS.**
- \`scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py\` — CI canary that FAILS if the faithfulness gate is bypassed. **All 4 checks pass.**
- \`scripts/cellcog_composer.py\` — gate now ON THE CRITICAL PATH; mechanism launder CLOSED (34 fabrications purged);
  FABRICATED_BINDING caught by name.
- Baseline pinned: rank10 = 0.4382 (k=5, judge SD 0.0074).
`;

const PLAN = {
  type: 'object',
  required: ['thesis', 'the_wheel', 'turns', 'integrity', 'generality', 'expected', 'kill_rules'],
  properties: {
    thesis: { type: 'string', description: 'Three sentences. What actually gets us from 0.4382 to >0.5603.' },
    the_wheel: { type: 'string', description: 'The loop: fix -> compose -> score (CRITERION level) -> read the judge -> fix. Concretely, with the scripts we already have.' },
    turns: {
      type: 'array',
      description: 'The ORDERED turns. Each is ONE executable unit of work with a measurable target.',
      items: {
        type: 'object',
        required: ['n', 'name', 'change', 'targets_criteria', 'expected_score_units', 'cheap_test', 'kill'],
        properties: {
          n: { type: 'number' },
          name: { type: 'string' },
          change: { type: 'string', description: 'Concrete. file:line. What code changes.' },
          targets_criteria: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'The EXACT rubric criteria this moves — we measure the lever on THESE, not on the scalar.' },
          expected_score_units: { type: 'string', description: 'In SCORE units (not raw criterion points). Price honestly.' },
          cheap_test: { type: 'string', description: 'Proves the mechanism FIRED before any judge call.' },
          kill: { type: 'string', description: 'What result kills this turn.' },
        },
      },
    },
    integrity: { type: 'string', description: 'The faithfulness contract, rewritten around VOICE (ATTRIBUTED vs OWNED). What ships, what is deleted, what the CI canary asserts.' },
    generality: { type: 'string', description: 'How we wire the domain packs and MEASURE generality (38 runs, all task 72 — we do not know). The cheapest test.' },
    expected: { type: 'string', description: 'Honest. Does the stack of turns beat 0.5603? If not, say how short and what is missing.' },
    kill_rules: { type: 'string', description: 'Pre-registered. What stops us. No re-narration.' },
  },
}

phase('Consolidate')
const ANGLES = [
  { k: 'score-first', q: 'Consolidate with ONE priority: the fastest path to a MEASURED score above 0.5603 on task 72. Order the turns by score-units-per-hour. Be ruthless about what is worth doing.' },
  { k: 'system-first', q: 'Consolidate with ONE priority: a GENERAL system. 38 scored runs, all task 72 — generality is UNMEASURED. Wire the domain packs. Make the answer-shape derived. A system that answers one question is a demo. But do NOT let this become an excuse to defer the score — sequence both.' },
  { k: 'integrity-first', q: 'Consolidate with ONE priority: the faithfulness architecture, rebuilt around the ATTRIBUTED/OWNED invariant. The old contract rejects 97% of the prose that scores 9.8/10. Design the contract that admits cellcog-class synthesis and still makes fabrication impossible — then fit the score plan around it.' },
]
const cons = await parallel(ANGLES.map(a => () =>
  agent(`${GROUND}

# YOUR ANGLE
${a.q}

# RULES
- **Everything here has already been ATTACKED.** Build on what SURVIVED; do not resurrect what DIED.
- **Price every lever in SCORE UNITS, not raw criterion points.** That units error was in our own brief and it
  inflated the headline lever 5x.
- **Every lever must name the CRITERIA it targets** — we now measure levers at the criterion level.
- Be honest about the bar: **6.35 -> 9.63/10 weighted, above cellcog's 9.38.** ~13 stacked levers.
- No proxies. Verify anything load-bearing.`,
    { schema: PLAN, effort: 'max', label: `consolidate:${a.k}`, phase: 'Consolidate' })
))
const C = cons.filter(Boolean)
log(`consolidators: ${C.length}/3`)

phase('Attack')
const V = { type: 'object', required: ['refuted', 'reasoning'], properties: { refuted: { type: 'boolean' }, reasoning: { type: 'string' }, fix: { type: 'string' } } }
const attacked = await parallel(C.map((c, i) => () =>
  agent(`Attack this consolidated plan. Default refuted=true if uncertain.

THESIS: ${c.thesis}
WHEEL: ${String(c.the_wheel).slice(0, 2500)}
TURNS: ${JSON.stringify(c.turns).slice(0, 6000)}
INTEGRITY: ${String(c.integrity).slice(0, 2000)}
EXPECTED: ${c.expected}

ATTACK ON ALL OF:
1. **ARITHMETIC**: do the turns, priced in SCORE units, actually sum to >0.5603? Remember R DROPS as we improve
   (proven), so every lever is worth ~20% more than an R-fixed model says. Check the sum honestly.
2. **DEAD LEVERS**: does it resurrect anything that DIED? (30+ subsections = well-powered null. Length beyond 8k =
   saturated. "Document shape is worthless" = a units error, but so was "+5.0 is the biggest lever".)
3. **FABRICATION**: under the ATTRIBUTED/OWNED contract, trace a concrete path by which a false claim ships.
4. **THE UNMEASURED**: does it actually MEASURE generality, or just assert it? 38 runs, all task 72.
5. **WASTE**: is any turn unmeasurable at the criterion level? A lever we cannot resolve is a lever we cannot keep.`,
    { schema: V, effort: 'high', label: `attack:${ANGLES[i].k}`, phase: 'Attack' })
    .then(v => ({ angle: ANGLES[i].k, plan: c, verdict: v }))
))
const A = attacked.filter(Boolean)
log(`attacks: ${A.filter(a => a.verdict?.refuted).length}/${A.length} refuted`)

phase('Final')
const final = await agent(
  `Three consolidators produced plans (score-first, system-first, integrity-first). Each was attacked.

${GROUND}

# THE THREE PLANS AND THEIR ATTACKS
${JSON.stringify(A.map(a => ({
    angle: a.angle, thesis: a.plan.thesis,
    turns: a.plan.turns, integrity: String(a.plan.integrity).slice(0, 2500),
    generality: String(a.plan.generality).slice(0, 1500),
    expected: a.plan.expected, kill: a.plan.kill_rules,
    attack: { refuted: a.verdict?.refuted, why: String(a.verdict?.reasoning).slice(0, 2500), fix: a.verdict?.fix },
  })), null, 1).slice(0, 110000)}

# WRITE THE ONE PLAN
Merge them. Where they conflict, DECIDE and justify. Where an attack landed, FIX or DROP.

It must be **THE WHEEL**: an ordered list of TURNS, each one executable, each naming the CRITERIA it targets, each
with a CHEAP TEST that proves the mechanism fired before a judge call, and each with a KILL condition.

Non-negotiable:
- **Turn 1 is already DONE**: the faithfulness gate is wired, the mechanism launder is closed, the canary is green.
- Price in SCORE units. Account for the R-drag (every lever is worth ~20% more than an R-fixed model says).
- Measure levers at the CRITERION level (scripts/criterion_ab.py). 20 of 25 criteria cannot clear a scalar rule.
- Rebuild the contract around **ATTRIBUTED vs OWNED**. The old one rejects 97% of 9.8/10 prose.
- **MEASURE generality** — do not assert it. 38 runs, all task 72.
- Be honest: does the stack beat 0.5603? **If not, say exactly how short and what is missing.** Do not re-target.`,
  { schema: PLAN, effort: 'max', label: 'the-one-plan', phase: 'Final' }
)
return { consolidations: C.map(c => c.thesis), attacks: A.map(a => ({ angle: a.angle, refuted: a.verdict?.refuted })), plan: final }

consolidations: The band jump is not mystical and it is not 13 independent levers — it is ONE artifact we already built and have never once run. `scripts/cellcog_composer.py` implements the journal-only corpus, in-prose attribution (the only citation form that survives RACE's cleaner), a rubric-derived outline with dedicated sections for the four "unnamed residual" criteria (4IR, sectoral scope, critical synthesis, foresight), ~100w paragraphs, H3 structure and epistemic labels — and `outputs/cellcog_arm/` is EMPTY. Three measured defects will gut it if we run it today: the OWNED gate rejects 83.5% of the #1 system's own 9.8/10 prose (47.6% on "any digit", 28.2% on entities checked against a 10-card subsection instead of the full ledger); the ATTRIBUTED lane — where every number and source name lives — has ZERO checking and the writer is never shown the span (numbers exist in 31/133 spans but only 16/133 claims); and `_SYNTH_CARDS` is a module global mutated by 6 concurrent threads, so the gate validates subsection A's prose against subsection B's cards. Fix those three (all $0, deterministic, testable without a judge call), merge the 120-DOI journal evidence set that already sits on disk read by nobody (32 → 146 works, vs cellcog's 88), then compose and score — that is the fastest path, and it is a coin-flip at best: I expect ~0.53, not >0.5603.

The architecture carrying every lever we built — in-prose attribution, ~100w paragraphs, H3s, epistemic labels — HAS NEVER BEEN SCORED (outputs/cellcog_arm/ was empty until today), and I found two mechanical defects silently eating it: cellcog_composer.py:391's splitter decapitates 45 of 135 attributed sentences at "et al." (subject survives as a fragment; the predicate — the actual FINDING — is thrown to the synthesis gate and deleted), and :403 leaves the ATTRIBUTED lane (where fabrication is fatal) COMPLETELY UNGATED while the OWNED lane (where non-entailment IS insight) rejects 489/506 = 97% of the exact prose the judge pays 9.6/9.8 for — the invariant is inverted in code. Second, and this is the unification: 64% of the rubric's weight is the task prompt's own clause list re-asked as questions (RACE's criteria generator is handed ONLY {task_prompt}, criteria_prompt_en.py:16), and those clause-clusters are 57% (+0.0783) of our gap to cellcog — so deriving the answer-shape from the prompt is NOT a generality tax, it is the single largest block of score. We get from 0.4382 to a contender by repairing the two defects eating the levers we already own, then deriving the shape, then growing the corpus that must fill it — landing honestly at 0.510–0.555: beats bodhi, falls short of cellcog, and the shortfall lives in CORPUS MASS, not prose.

The contract is not merely too strict — it is INVERTED, and I proved it end-to-end through production code: `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py:403` fires the gate ONLY on sentences that do NOT match `[Ww]riting in the`, so ATTRIBUTED sentences (name a source; where fabrication is fraud) are 100% UNGATED while OWNED sentences (the reviewer's voice; explicitly permitted to be non-entailed) are the only ones checked — by rules that reject 97% of the prose the judge scored 9.8/10. The exact fabricated binding the CI canary exists to catch is REJECTED in the canary's phrasing and ADMITTED when phrased in the form `WRITE_PROMPT:296` MANDATES; all 4 canary checks pass and it prints "GATE IS WIRED AND ON THE CRITICAL PATH." Fixing the inversion is simultaneously the safety fix, the biggest score lever on the board (the INSIGHT dimension, w=0.32, is entirely non-entailed relations — the class we delete — worth +0.0463, 39% of the gap), and the generality fix (VERDICT_VOCAB is 17 hardcoded English idioms and SAFE_CAPS hardcodes `AI/Artificial/Intelligence/Fourth/Industrial/Revolution` — task-72 overfit sitting inside the safety gate, rejecting 100% of Chinese prose across 50 of the 100 tasks).

plan.thesis: THE ARM HAS ALREADY BEEN COMPOSED AND SCORED — 0.4203, A LOSS — and all three plans book +0.06..+0.09 on running it. `outputs/judge_feedback_turn2.json` and commit 20471a6 have been on disk since 17:45 and nobody opened them. Inside that loss is the most valuable measurement we own: the arm is TWO SEPARABLE EFFECTS SUPERPOSED — the citation channel is INTERVENTIONALLY CONFIRMED (+0.546 weighted T; 'Exclusive Citation of High-Quality Journal Articles' 1.5 -> 7.5, the largest single criterion move in project history) and a coverage collapse (-0.899 weighted T across 16 criteria) paid for it and then some. The judge's OWN WORDS attribute that collapse to "incomplete sentences", "missing words", "missing completions", "abrupt or incomplete claims", "truncated", "extremely brief" — and I verified those are a LIVE SENTENCE-DECAPITATION BUG: 45 sentences on the scored page end at "et al." and 42 orphan predicate-fragments ("show that organizations only achieve...") were SHIPPED TO THE JUDGE, because the splitter at cellcog_composer.py:391 cuts on the period in "et al." So the wheel is: repair the destruction, KEEP the channel, buy DEPTH PER SOURCE (not breadth), and argue the 4IR framing — which is 24.5% of the entire residual and pure composition. Honestly: that lands at ~0.49-0.52. It decisively beats our 0.4382 baseline. It does NOT beat bodhi (0.5399) and does NOT beat cellcog (0.5587).
plan.the_wheel:
FIX -> DETERMINISTIC PRE-CLEANER COUNTER ($0, proves the mechanism fired) -> COMPOSE -> criterion_ab.py --targets AT k=5 (measure the lever on ITS OWN CRITERIA, never on the scalar) -> judge_feedback.py (READ THE JUDGE'S WRITTEN ANALYSIS — it names its own reasons in prose) -> FIX.

TURN 1 IS DONE AND I VERIFIED IT. `python3 scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` prints "** GATE IS WIRED AND ON THE CRITICAL PATH **"; all 4 checks PASS; FABRICATED_BINDING catches by name the live fabrication ("task displacement" is stated by Autor, not by Bresnahan — the sentence credits the wrong paper). The gate is on the critical path at cellcog_composer.py:403-407. Do not re-plan it.

THE SCRIPTS THAT MAKE THE WHEEL TURN (all exist, all verified runnable):
- scripts/judge_feedback.py — THE ORACLE. Captures per-criterion scores AND the judge's written analysis for BOTH articles. It is how we stopped guessing. NOTE (all three plans got this wrong): it constructs `ArticleCleaner(AIClient())` at :80 and calls `chunk_clean_article` at :81 — THE POST-CLEANER COUNT IS AN LLM CALL, NOT $0. Every cheap test below is therefore a PRE-cleaner deterministic regex counter.
- scripts/criterion_ab.py --a <base> --b <arm> --task-id 72 --k 5 --targets "<substrings>" — criterion-level A/B. This is where the power is: 20 of 25 criteria cannot clear a scalar kill rule even at a perfect 10/10.
- scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py — the CI canary. Extended in Turn 2 with the poisoned-ATTRIBUTED-card check.

THE MEASUREMENT DISCIPLINE, RESTATED AS A RULE: a score run is INADMISSIBLE AS EVIDENCE about a lever whose $0 counter did not move. The counter runs FIRST. If it is flat we spend nothing on the judge. This repo's history is levers that moved a metric while the mechanism never fired — and levers that fired and were thrown away because a scalar could not resolve them.

THE DRAG LAW, PINNED (this is load-bearing and I re-derived it from the judge's own output):
  R(T) = 8.040 - 0.211*(T - 6.283)
  Two-point line through our MEASURED (T=6.283, R=8.040) and cellcog's MEASURED (T=9.358, R=7.390). EXACT at both anchors. The within-system estimate from the arm (dR/dT = -0.388) is n=1, local, and measured on a T DECREASE — do not extrapolate it upward.
  dS/dT = [R - T*R'] / (T+R)^2 = 0.0457 at our operating point. EVERY LEVER BELOW IS PRICED AT THIS.
  AND THE FACT THAT CHANGES EVERYTHING: with R FROZEN at our 8.040, a PERFECT 10/10 article scores 10/18.04 = 0.5543 — BELOW cellcog's 0.5587. THE DRAG IS NOT A BONUS. IT IS A REQUIREMENT. There is no path to the target that does not also drag the reference down, and the only way to do that is to actually be good.

turns.name: THE VOICE ROUTER + SENTENCE INTEGRITY — recompose, and score. (This is the make-or-break turn: it banks the confirmed channel and undoes the collapse that paid for it.)
turns.change:
FOUR DEFECTS, ALL VERIFIED IN LIVE CODE, ALL ON THE SCORED PAGE.

(a) scripts/cellcog_composer.py:391 — `sents = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', p)`. 'et al.' ENDS IN A PERIOD. MEASURED ON THE SCORED ARTIFACT (outputs/cellcog_arm/report.md): 45 sentences terminate at 'et al.' (35 of the 91 'Writing in' sentences), and 42 ORPHAN PREDICATE-FRAGMENTS SHIPPED TO THE JUDGE — verbatim: 'show that organizations only achieve performance gains from AI capabilities if they use these techno...'. Fix: abbreviation-aware splitter protecting `et al.`, `e.g.`, `i.e.`, `cf.`, `vs.`, `Fig.`, and initials.

(b) scripts/cellcog_composer.py:324 — DELETE the `_SYNTH_CARDS` module global. It is written under `global` at :434-435 inside `one()`, which runs on `ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=6)` at :441, and read at :403. SIX THREADS RACE: the gate validates subsection A's prose against subsection B's cards. Pass `sel` explicitly: `_clean(raw, sel)`.

(c) cellcog_composer.py:403 — THE INVERSION. `if _SYNTH_CARDS and not re.search(r'[Ww]riting in the|,\s+in the\s+\*', s) and len(s.split()) > 8:` — any sentence matching 'Writing in the' BYPASSES THE GATE ENTIRELY, and so does any sentence of <=8 words. That is the ATTRIBUTED lane — where every number and every author name lives, and where fabrication is FRAUD — and it has ZERO checking today. Replace the regex with a VOICE ROUTER keyed on the ledger's real author/venue surface forms. Then GATE THE ATTRIBUTED LANE: every number in a sentence naming source S must appear in S's OWN VERBATIM SPAN; every mechanism must be in S's span-gated `mechanisms`; the named source must be in this subsection's premise set. Reject on NEUTRAL *and* CONTRADICTED — here and only here.

(d) cellcog_composer.py:345-354 `_fmt_cards` — :350 hands the writer `FINDING: {c['claim']}`, the LLM's own paraphrase (the extraction prompt at :118 literally says 'in your words'). THE SPAN IS NEVER SHOWN. Consequence, measured: numbers live in 31/133 SPANS but only 16/133 CLAIMS — ATTRIBUTED sentences are structurally number-starved. Emit the verbatim SPAN.

OWNED LANE, scripts/synthesis_contract.py: rule 4 `synthesis_carries_a_digit` (NUMERIC) -> 'no NEW number' (every numeric token must already be in the LEDGER). I MEASURED IT: the any-digit rule alone rejects 48.1% of cellcog's 543 body sentences — 48 of the 50 points of false rejection. It is a ROUTING error, not a rule error: those sentences name sources and belong in the ATTRIBUTED lane. Make the entity check LEDGER-scoped, not premise-scoped. DELETE FORECAST (:244) and UNIVERSAL (:246) — together only 2.7%, but FORECAST bans 'will' and makes 'Value and Foresight' (w=.0640, we score 4.5, judge: 'truncated') LITERALLY UNWRITABLE. KEEP rule 10 `not_anchored_in_its_premises` and KEEP FABRICATED_BINDING.

THE TWO SELF-INFLICTED WOUNDS THE JUDGE NAMED, BOTH FREE: cap any single attribution template at <=3 uses ('Writing in' x91; judge: 'repeated phrases such as "Writing in" ... SEVERELY reduce clarity'), and RESTORE THE REFERENCE LIST (judge, twice: 'the article lacks a formal reference list, making verification difficult'; rank10 HAS one and drew no such complaint).

scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py — add CHECK #5: a poisoned ATTRIBUTED card ('Writing in the American Economic Review in 2018, Acemoglu and Restrepo show that robots raised wages by 62 percent' — real journal, real authors, INVENTED number) must be REJECTED, and the canary must FAIL on a report containing it.

F1: Formatting, Layout, and Visual Consistency (w=.0200; arm 5.5 — judge: 'missing completions, no formal reference list')
S2: Paragraph Cohesion and Transitions (w=.0200; arm 4.2 — judge: 'abrupt or incomplete claims')
P1: Clarity and Synthesis in Presenting Sourced Information (w=.0200; arm 5.0)
Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles' (w=.0357; arm 7.5 — MUST NOT REGRESS; this is the banked lever)

Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis (w=.0483; arm 5.2 — judge: 'shallow and often mismatched to the headings')
turns.expected_score_units: +0.010 to +0.030 vs the pinned 0.4382 baseline (landing 0.448 - 0.468). PRICED HONESTLY AND NOT INFLATED: the arm currently sits at 0.4203, so this turn must first climb +0.018 back to par before it banks anything. It does NOT contain a +0.06 shot; that number was booked by all three plans against an artifact that has already been scored and LOST. What it does contain is the ONLY interventionally-confirmed lever we own (+0.546 weighted T on the citation cluster) plus the removal of the destruction that swamped it. `sections:` list that is read NOWHERE; and `multi_section_generator.py:785 _ALLOWED_SECTIONS` hardcodes a
drug-trial genre (Efficacy/Safety/Regulatory/Dose Response) while `:811-818` routes a BLANK domain to the
CLINICAL set, contradicting the two modules that declare 'blank -> general, NEVER clinical'.

*** BUT IT BUYS ZERO SCORE AND ZERO GENERALITY ON THE ARM WE SHIP, AND I WILL NOT LET IT LOOK OTHERWISE.
cellcog_composer.py IMPORTS NEITHER multi_section_generator NOR domain_pack. It carries its OWN hardcoded
`OUTLINE` at :233-282. Wiring the packs fixes a loaded footgun on a code path this arm does not execute, and
it does not fire on the benchmark anyway (all 18 SWEEP_QUERIES carry a domain). DO IT OUT OF BAND, FOR
CORRECTNESS, with a golden test that `_allowed_sections_for_domain('')` contains no clinical genre label.
PRICED AT ZERO. NOT ON THE CRITICAL PATH. ***

THE REAL OVERFIT ON THE SHIPPING PATH IS TWO CONSTANTS:
  cellcog_composer.py:233-282  — a hand-written 10-section/27-subsection OUTLINE derived from task 72's own
                                 criteria (its own comment at :219-231 admits it)
  cellcog_composer.py:102-114  — EXTRACT_PROMPT hardcodes 'the restructuring impact of Artificial
                                 Intelligence on the labor market'

HOW WE WIRE IT (Turn 7): the GENRE skeleton comes from `load_domain_pack(domain)['sections']`; the TOPIC and
the clause list are derived from the task prompt alone. This is the one place where generality and score are
plausibly the same lever — RACE's criteria generator is handed ONLY `{task_prompt}` (criteria_prompt_en.py:16),
so the rubric IS the prompt's own clause list re-asked as questions. But I do not book that as score, because
the composer DROPS a derived subsection that matches no cards (`one()` returns '' when `_select` is empty) —
so a derived outline cannot CREATE coverage, it can only re-partition the 133 cards we hold. Turn 4 owns
coverage; Turn 7 owns generality. They are not the same turn and pretending they are is a double-count.

THE CHEAPEST TEST, AND IT IS THE WHOLE POINT (~$3, Turn 7): COMPOSE AND SCORE END-TO-END ON TASKS 78 AND 90.
Nobody has ever done this. The headline metric becomes the 3-TASK PANEL MEAN (72 academic / 78 clinical /
90 proposal). K4 IS A HARD GATE: a lever that lifts 72 and moves nothing on 78/90 is an OVERFIT and is
REVERTED, not tweaked.

AND THE GENERALITY FACT THAT IS ALSO A SAFETY FACT: every reject-trigger in the contract is an ENGLISH REGEX.
On Chinese prose they match NOTHING and therefore ADMIT. Rule 10 (`not_anchored_in_its_premises`) is the only
rule that can fire at all — which is why one of the three plans' proposal to DELETE it would make the contract
fail OPEN on 50 of the 100 benchmark tasks. K8 stands: 50 of 100 tasks are Chinese, nothing we have built
composes in Chinese, and EVERY corpus-wide claim in this plan is scoped to the 50 ENGLISH TASKS or retracted
on sight.
plan.expected:
NO. THE STACK DOES NOT BEAT 0.5603. It lands at ~0.49-0.52. It does not even beat bodhi (0.5399). Here is the
arithmetic, every input a MEASURED per-criterion score from the judge's own output, with the drag law pinned by
two measured anchors.

  THE DRAG LAW:  R(T) = 8.040 - 0.211*(T - 6.283)   [exact at our measured point AND at cellcog's]
  dS/dT = 0.0457 at our operating point.

  BASELINE (pinned, k=5)                                          0.4382     (recompute: 0.4387, T=6.283)
  THE ARM AS ALREADY SCORED — nobody read this                    0.4203     (T=5.929, R=8.177)  A LOSS
  T2  voice router + sentence integrity + reflist + templates    +0.010..0.030
  T3  the 4IR argument                                           +0.008..0.020
  T4  depth per source (deep-fetch; HIGHEST VARIANCE)            +0.010..0.025
  T5  declared constraint made true + contradiction census       +0.006..0.014
  T6  eight named tagged syntheses                               +0.006..0.012
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  REALISTIC LANDING                                               0.48 .. 0.52
  BODHI                                                           0.5399   (needs T = 8.894)
  CELLCOG                                                         0.5587   (needs T = 9.358)
  P(panel mean > bodhi)   ~ 20-25%.    P(panel mean > cellcog) ~ 5-10%.

HOW SHORT, EXACTLY: ~0.04 to ~0.07 score units = 1.5 to 1.8 RAW WEIGHTED POINTS on a 10-point scale. To reach
0.5587 we need T = 9.358 — WHICH IS CELLCOG'S OWN T. There is no subset of criteria that gets there. All 25
criteria taken to cellcog's own scores lands on EXACTLY 0.5587. We must match the #1 system on essentially
everything. cellcog is not winning on a trick; it is an outstanding literature review on every axis.

WHAT IS MISSING, NAMED EXACTLY — I ranked the residual after the full stack:

1. DEPTH PER SOURCE (~76% of the residual, and it is not composable). We hold 133 verified spans over 32
   works = 4.16 items/work; only 31 of 133 spans carry a NUMBER at all; 24 of 36 fetched papers are
   ABSTRACT-ONLY. The composer has ~28 subsections and 133 cards — 4.75 cards per subsection WITH REUSE —
   which is exactly why the judge wrote 'the healthcare and education section MOSTLY REPEATS FREY AND OSBORNE
   rather than analyzing.' CELLCOG READ ITS ~96 PAPERS. WE HOLD ONE QUOTE FROM MOST OF OURS. You cannot score
   9.7 on 'Analytical Depth in Characterizing Mechanisms' or 9.6 on 'Critical Synthesis' from one quote per
   paper. No prompt fixes that. Turn 4 is the only lever that touches it and its yield is genuinely unknown —
   which is why it launches in the background at minute zero and why K-DEPTH is the kill rule most likely to
   fire.

2. THE 4IR ARGUMENT (24.5% of the residual, and it IS composable — this is the one thing I found that no plan
   headlined). The three 4IR criteria carry a COMBINED WEIGHT OF 0.1480 — 14.8% of the entire rubric. We
   score 5.8 / 6.5 / 6.7; cellcog scores 9.2 / 9.4 / 9.3. Taken to its level: +0.451 raw T = +0.0206 score
   units, 2.2x the resolvable effect, FROM ONE CONCEPTUAL SECTION. And we have been gaming the wrong metric
   by accident: rank10 says '4IR' 18 times against bodhi's 8 and still loses by 2+ points. The judge is not
   counting mentions; it wants a conceptual system and we have never supplied one.

THE ONE THING THAT COULD MAKE ME WRONG IN THE GOOD DIRECTION, AND THE ONE THAT COULD MAKE ME WRONG IN THE BAD:
  GOOD: the citation channel is the only INTERVENTIONALLY CONFIRMED lever we own (+0.546 weighted T; journal
  criterion 1.5 -> 7.5, the largest single criterion move in project history) and it was measured THROUGH a
  grammatically destroyed page — 45 decapitated sentences and 42 orphan fragments. Its clean value may be
  materially higher than the +0.0158 I book.
  BAD: with R FROZEN at our current 8.040, a PERFECT 10/10 ARTICLE SCORES 0.5543 — BELOW CELLCOG. The drag is
  not a bonus, it is a REQUIREMENT, and the whole plan's arithmetic rests on a two-point line. If dR/dT is
  materially weaker than -0.211, the target is unreachable at ANY T and we should say so rather than discover
  it at turn 6. Re-measure it at k=5 in Turn 2 at no extra cost, since we are already scoring.

I AM NOT GOING TO RE-TARGET, AND I AM NOT GOING TO DRESS THIS UP. The plan roughly doubles our distance from
the baseline toward bodhi and stops well short of both rivals. That is the honest expectation.
plan.kill_rules:
PRE-REGISTERED. NO RE-NARRATION AFTER THE NUMBER LANDS.

K-COUNTER (every turn, non-negotiable). A SCORE RUN IS INADMISSIBLE AS EVIDENCE ABOUT A LEVER WHOSE $0
DETERMINISTIC COUNTER DID NOT MOVE. The counter runs FIRST. No counter, no claim, no judge call. This repo's
history is a graveyard of levers that moved a metric while the mechanism never fired. (And the counters are
PRE-CLEANER regex: judge_feedback.py:80 builds ArticleCleaner(AIClient()) — the post-cleaner count is an LLM
call. All three plans priced it at $0 and all three were wrong.)

K-CRITERION (every turn). A lever is judged on THE CRITERIA IT TARGETS via criterion_ab.py --targets, NEVER on
the scalar. 20 of 25 criteria cannot clear +0.0094 even at a perfect 10/10. IF THE TARGETED CRITERION MOVES AND
THE SCALAR DOES NOT, THE CRITERION WINS. Deciding a lever on the scalar is a category error and it is how good
work gets deleted here.

K-ARM (Turn 2). The repaired arm scores BELOW rank10's pinned 0.4382 at k=5 -> THE COMPOSER THESIS IS DEAD.
Do not tweak the composer for a second turn. Revert to rank10 and backfill (author, year, venue) onto its
105-source ledger instead — that counterfactual is worth +0.0158 by measured per-criterion arithmetic.

K-GATE (Turn 2). MUST-ADMIT on cellcog's 543 body sentences < 85%, OR ANY deterministic MUST-REJECT leaks ->
STOP. Do not compose. NEVER weaken the ATTRIBUTED lane to make the OWNED lane's numbers look better.

K-SPAN (Turn 2). Enforcing span-entailment drops >40% of ATTRIBUTED sentences -> the extraction CLAIMS are not
faithful to their spans. FIX EXTRACTION. Relaxing the check is FORBIDDEN; that direction is precisely how 43%
of our mechanisms became inventions.

K-CANARY (Turn 2). If the canary passes while a MUST-REJECT fixture reaches the page through the real entry
point, THE CANARY IS VOID AND THE TURN IS FAILED. Today it prints 'GATE IS WIRED AND ON THE CRITICAL PATH'
with all 4 checks green while the ATTRIBUTED lane is 100% ungated. A test that certifies the gate is wired to
the WRONG LANE is worse than no test.

K-DEPTH (Turn 4) — THE ONE MOST LIKELY TO FIRE. OA recovery < 25% -> the depth ceiling is REAL, not a wiring
failure. Re-price the plan DOWNWARD IN WRITING and stop claiming we can match a system that read its sources.

K-CORPUS (Turn 4), NEW AND ABSOLUTE. corpus_journal_evidence.json is NEVER merged as EVIDENCE. Measured: 1.21
items/work (takes depth 4.16 -> 1.90, a 54% regression on the binding axis), 10 provenance-corrupt spans
(Substack CDN chrome; a blog author-bio bound to a real journal DOI with real Crossref authors), plus
ImageNet/ResNet and PRISMA x3. Its Crossref metadata may be used as an ATTRIBUTION INDEX only. A work enters
the CITABLE ledger only if we hold >=3 verified spans from it.

K-DECLARE (Turn 5). If the admitted pool is not journal-only and English-only, THE DECLARATION IS SCOPED TO
WHAT IS TRUE. A declaration we cannot honor is precisely the fabrication we are trying to kill — and we have
already shipped it once.

K4 / PANEL (Turn 7, hard CI gate). A lever that lifts task 72 and moves nothing on 78/90 is an OVERFIT and is
REVERTED, not tweaked.

K-RUN. Two consecutive turns gaining < +0.0094 on the panel mean while below target -> STOP AND REPORT MISSED.
There is no fallback success state. A plan that ships with a fallback hits the fallback.

K8 / HONESTY. 50 of the 100 tasks are Chinese and nothing we have built composes in Chinese. Every corpus-wide
claim is scoped to the 50 English tasks or retracted on sight.

K-ABORT (overrides everything above, including every score in this plan). One fabricated particular on a
shipped page burns the artifact regardless of score. A 0.60 obtained by fabricating is a 0.00.

DEAD, AND THEY STAY DEAD:
- K1, THE CEILING GATE. It FIRES TODAY at R=8.033 and declares a ceiling of 0.5545 that cellcog's MEASURED
  0.5603 already exceeds. It measured R against our own weakness and concluded the target was impossible.
- 'ADD 30+ SUBSECTIONS' (+0.0020/SD, a well-powered null; the H3 r=+0.519 is 100% system identity) and the
  13,500-16,500w length targets (length saturates ~8,000w; rank10 is at 9,300, past the knee).
- 'COMPOSE THE ARM AND WIN +0.06..0.09' — ALL THREE PLANS BOOK THIS. THE ARM HAS BEEN SCORED. IT WENT 0.4382
  -> 0.4203. THE CLAIM IS REFUTED BY MEASUREMENT SITTING ON DISK.
- 'THE CONTRACT REJECTS 97% OF THE 9.8/10 PROSE' — inflated; it counts VERDICT_VOCAB, which is DEAD CODE (0
  uses in validate()). The live rejection is 50.1%, and 48 of those 50 points are the any-digit rule, which is
  a ROUTING error. Any lever booked on deleting VERDICT_VOCAB, FORECAST or UNIVERSAL is worth ~2.7%, not 39%
  of the gap.
- 'STRUCTURE IS PROVEN WORTHLESS' (commit 20471a6). CONFOUNDED, and I am reopening it narrowly. The same
  intervention that split the paragraphs ALSO shipped 45 decapitated sentences and 42 orphan fragments, and
  the judge's L1/F1 complaints quote that damage verbatim. S2 — the criterion the paragraph split actually
  TARGETS — moved +0.7, and P1 moved +1.0. The SECTION/H3 null stands. The PARAGRAPH lever is UNMEASURED, not
  dead, and Turn 2 measures it cleanly for the first time.
